title: Functional Safety Procedure
preamble: This procedure defines requirements for managing Safety Instrumented Systems (SIS) and other functional safety systems to ensure they remain capable of performing their intended safety function throughout their lifecycle — from design through commissioning, operation, maintenance, modification, and decommissioning.
owns: Defeat and bypass management for SIS
references: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0021 – Permit to Work (SIS testing via PTW)
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0024 – Isolation (isolation of SIS for testing)
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0040 – Management of Change (SIS changes via MoC)
referenced_by: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0019 references RTPR-HSE-PRO-0020 for SIS testing
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0047 references RTPR-HSE-PRO-0020 for pre-commissioning safety system testing

requirements.tag: [RT – RT – Process Safety Standard and Functional Safety Standard]
requirements.text: Define functional safety lifecycle requirements from concept through decommissioning. Require Safety Integrity Level (SIL) determination for all safety functions based on risk assessment. Require documented Safety Requirements Specifications (SRS) for each safety function before detailed design begins.
Ensure that SIS and other safety instrumented functions are designed by competent personnel and that the design basis is documented and retained for the life of the system.

The project must define the methodology for SIL determination (e.g., risk graph, layer of protection analysis), the authority level required to approve SIL assignments, and the process for reviewing and confirming SIL allocations at each project stage gate.

Require Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT) and Site Acceptance Testing (SAT) for all SIS before commissioning, with results documented and retained. Require commissioning documentation confirming each safety function has been tested and verified before it is relied upon for plant protection.

The project must define the minimum documentation requirements for SIS handover from the construction team to the operations team, ensuring that as-built documentation, test results, and proof test procedures are transferred and controlled.

Require a formal authorisation process for any defeat or bypass of a safety instrumented function: risk assessment before bypass; documented compensating controls; time-limited bypass with defined duration; approval at appropriate authority level; and active tracking of all active bypasses.
Ensure that bypass of any critical control is captured in the project action tracking system and reported to the project owner as part of monthly HSESC reporting.

Define proof test interval requirements based on the SIL target for each safety function. Require that proof test procedures are documented, followed by competent personnel, and that results are documented and any failures investigated.

The project must define the minimum functional safety competency requirements for personnel responsible for SIS testing and maintenance, and document the process for verifying that contractors engaged in SIS work meet those requirements.

project_completion_guidance: Define the SIL determination methodology approved for this project (risk graph, LOPA, or equivalent) and insert the approved methodology or reference to the project-specific process safety study.
For each safety instrumented function on this project, complete the SRS before detailed design is finalised. Define the SRS template to be used.
Define the project FAT and SAT requirements: what systems require FAT; who witnesses FAT/SAT; what documentation is required. Insert as a table.
Define the functional safety competency requirements for this project: who must have formal TÜV or equivalent functional safety certification; who may perform proof tests under supervision only.

external_sources.clause: Process Safety Standard and Functional Safety Standard
external_sources.summary: Requires functional safety lifecycle management from concept through decommissioning, including SIL determination, Safety Requirements Specifications, FAT/SAT, bypass management, and proof test intervals for all SIS.

Requires that SIS and safety instrumented functions are designed by competent personnel with documented design basis retained for the life of the system, and that adequate ventilation and confined space management practices are maintained.
Requires that any bypass of a critical control is captured in the project action tracking system and reported to the project owner as part of monthly HSESC reporting.
